relationships.relationship_description: Multi-year, multi-generation agreement to deploy AMD GPUs.
relationships.original_sentence: This morning AMD and META announced a multi-year, multi-generation agreement to deploy 6GW of AMD GPUs ( link), with initial capacity roll-outs scheduled for 2H26.

relationships.relationship_description: Compute capacity agreement, similar to META's deal.
relationships.original_sentence: Similar to the compute capacity agreement struck with OpenAI in October 2025 ( link), AMD’s agreement with META includes the issuance of warrants for up to 160MM AMD shares that will vest over time, based on META scaling Instinct GPU deployments to 6GW, as well as key technical and commercial milestones.

relationships.relationship_description: Commences MI450 deployments, contributing incremental revenue.
relationships.original_sentence: In our estimation, though, the OAI and META deals alone already set the stage for AMD to potentially grow DC GPU revenue well in excess of current expectations for CY27 and beyond, without even layering in much incremental revenue from other already-disclosed customers such as ORCL (which commences MI450 deployments in 3Q26).

relationships.relationship_description: Will, in aggregate with OAI, likely account for the vast majority of AMD’s DC GPU revenue and approach 50% of AMD’s total revenue in CY27.
relationships.quantifier: vast majority of AMD’s DC GPU revenue, approach 50% of AMD’s total revenue (in aggregate)
relationships.original_sentence: Considering META and OAI will, in aggregate, likely account for the vast majority of AMD’s DC GPU revenue in CY27 (and in turn approach 50% of AMD’s total revenue), we remain cautious on the outlook for GM, which the Street is currently modeling to be approximately flat Y/Y at ~54.9% for CY27.

relationships.relationship_description: Will, in aggregate with META, likely account for the vast majority of AMD’s DC GPU revenue in CY27.
relationships.quantifier: vast majority of AMD’s DC GPU revenue (in aggregate)
relationships.original_sentence: Considering META and OAI will, in aggregate, likely account for the vast majority of AMD’s DC GPU revenue in CY27 (and in turn approach 50% of AMD’s total revenue), we remain cautious on the outlook for GM, which the Street is currently modeling to be approximately flat Y/Y at ~54.9% for CY27.

relationships.relationship_description: Lead customer for 6th-gen “Venice” CPUs, expanding server CPU footprint.
relationships.original_sentence: AMD is poised to expand its server CPU footprint at META with this agreement (mgmt called out META as a lead customer for its 6th-gen “Venice” CPUs launching later this year), and more broadly, is positioned to continue to capture share of the broader server CPU market relative to INTC over the next few quarters, given its stronger product portfolio/roadmap and, in the near-term, a less challenging wafer supply backdrop (vs. INTC).

relationships.relationship_description: AMD is positioned to capture share of the broader server CPU market relative to INTC.
relationships.original_sentence: AMD is poised to expand its server CPU footprint at META with this agreement (mgmt called out META as a lead customer for its 6th-gen “Venice” CPUs launching later this year), and more broadly, is positioned to continue to capture share of the broader server CPU market relative to INTC over the next few quarters, given its stronger product portfolio/roadmap and, in the near-term, a less challenging wafer supply backdrop (vs. INTC).
